title: App Styles (App.css)
content:
This file contains the core CSS styling for the DocMed application.

## Purpose

`App.css` defines the visual appearance and layout of the DocMed application, including:

- **Global styles:** Resets, root variables for theming (dark and light modes), and base body styles.
- **Layout components:** Styles for the overall application layout, main content area, and sidebar.
- **UI elements:** Styling for tabs, buttons, search input, navigation items, file tree, markdown rendering, and more.
- **Theming:** CSS variables are used extensively to support dark and light themes, allowing for easy color customization.

## Key Styles and Variables

### Color Palette (CSS Variables)

**Dark Theme (`:root` and `[data-theme="dark"]`)**

- `--bg`: Background color (#0b0d14)
- `--surface`: Surface color for cards, headers, etc. (#111520)
- `--surface-2`: Secondary surface color (#161b28)
- `--border`: Border color (#1e2535)
- `--border-2`: Secondary border color (#252f42)
- `--text`: Primary text color (#e2e8f0)
- `--text-2`: Secondary text color (#94a3b8)
- `--text-3`: Tertiary text color (#475569)
- `--accent`: Primary accent color for active states, links, etc. (#22d3ee)
- `--accent-dim`: Dimmed accent color for backgrounds (#rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.12))
- `--sidebar-w`: Sidebar width (240px)
- `--radius`: Border radius (8px)

**Light Theme (`[data-theme="light"]`)**

- `--bg`: Background color (#f8fafc)
- `--surface`: Surface color (#ffffff)
- `--surface-2`: Secondary surface color (#f1f5f9)
- `--border`: Border color (#e2e8f0)
- `--border-2`: Secondary border color (#cbd5e1)
- `--text`: Primary text color (#0f172a)
- `--text-2`: Secondary text color (#475569)
- `--text-3`: Tertiary text color (#94a3b8)
- `--accent`: Primary accent color (#0891b2)
- `--accent-dim`: Dimmed accent color (#rgba(8, 145, 178, 0.1))

### Layout Styles

- `.layout`: Establishes a flex container for the main layout (sidebar + main content).
- `.main-column`: Styles the main content area to take remaining space and stack vertically.

### Component Styles

- **Tab Bar (`.tab-bar`, `.tab-list`, `.tab`, `.tab-close`, `.tab-add`)**: Styles for managing multiple open document tabs.
- **Sidebar (`.sidebar`, `.sidebar.collapsed`, `.sidebar-top`, `.repo-title`, `.sidebar-top-actions`, `.theme-toggle`, `.collapse-btn`, `.sidebar-body`)**: Styles for the navigation sidebar, including collapse functionality.
- **Search (`.search-wrap`, `.search-icon`, `.search-input`, `.search-clear`)**: Styles for the file search input.
- **Navigation (`.nav`, `.nav-item`, `.nav-badge`, `.nav-docs`, `.nav-section-label`, `.nav-empty`)**: Styles for navigation lists, often used in the sidebar.
- **File Tree (`.tree-level`, `.tree-row`, `.tree-folder-row`, `.tree-chevron-btn`, `.tree-folder-label`, `.tree-chevron`, `.tree-icon`, `.tree-name`, `.tree-children`, `.tree-folder-group`)**: Styles for recursively displaying the file and folder structure.
- **Main Content (`.main`, `.page`, `.page-title`, `.page-subtitle`)**: Styles for the primary content area where documents are displayed.
- **Stats (`.stats-row`, `.stat-card`, `.stat-value`, `.stat-label`)**: Styles for displaying key statistics.
- **Home Sections (`.section-title`, `.file-grid`, `.file-card`, `.file-card-name`, `.file-card-path`, `.file-card-title`, `.home-section`)**: Styles specific to the home page sections.
- **Commits (`.commit-list`, `.commit-item`, `.commit-sha`, `.commit-body`, `.commit-msg`, `.commit-meta`)**: Styles for displaying commit information.
- **Doc Page (`.doc-filepath`)**: Styles for the file path displayed on documentation pages.
- **Markdown (`.markdown-body`)**: Styles for rendering markdown content, including headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and tables.
- **Misc (`.empty-state`, `.loading-pulse`)**: Styles for empty states and loading indicators.
- **Breadcrumb (`.breadcrumb`, `.breadcrumb-item`, `.breadcrumb-sep`, `.breadcrumb-current`, `.breadcrumb-link`)**: Styles for breadcrumb navigation.

## Usage

This CSS file is intended to be imported and used globally within the React application (likely in `src/index.js` or `src/App.js`). Developers can leverage the defined CSS variables to customize theme colors or extend styles by targeting the provided class names.
